# Principle 2: See Like an Agent


> **Keep the bar high for adding new tools. Always look for ways to add capability without adding tools.**

Claude Code currently ships with around 20 tools. Anthropic keeps that number deliberately low. Thariq is explicit: "The bar to add a new tool is high, because this gives the model one more option to think about."

Every tool you add forces Claude to consider that option on every turn. With 20 tools, Claude processes 20 options every decision. More tools can degrade judgment quality rather than improve it.

**Adding capability without adding tools via Progressive Disclosure** (→ Principle 1): In the Claude Code Guide Subagent case, Anthropic added "how to use Claude Code" as a capability without creating any new tool. They built a subagent Claude can call when needed. The action space expanded; the tool count did not.

**Bad**: "Claude needs to call our internal API, so let's create a `call_internal_api` tool" — without first asking whether a Skill plus a script could handle it.

**Good**: Include an API-calling script (`scripts/call_api.sh`) in the Skill and have Claude run it via Bash. If that's insufficient, then consider a dedicated tool.
